what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a comment on another board item) in which Tim agrees with a prior observation that three separate systems mirror each other's shapes and concludes they should be unified into one.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, board address, type=note, source=claude_code, state=posted, title 'Tim · paragraph', author_session=tim, channel=dragnet-development.
A links entry records commented_on target board://item-11e84d01.
History shows a single 'filed' transition at the created/updated timestamp.
Body is a short markdown paragraph quoting the earlier tension and stating 'Yep this is bad, they should only be one.'
standouts: Explicit architectural judgment: three mirrored systems 'should only be one'.
References a prior naming tension around things called 'channel' writing to different places.
Filed as a comment on another noticeboard item rather than a standalone decision record.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with frontmatter, board:// URI, type/state/source fields, links array, and history array.
Uses a [paragraph] marker prefix in the markdown body.
Created and updated timestamps are identical, consistent with an initial filing.
concerns: The conclusion is stated without supporting rationale, alternatives, or a migration plan.
The three systems and their stores are not identified, so the note is not actionable from this file alone.
It records an opinion rather than a recorded decision or accepted design change.
notes: Tim's note agreeing that three mirrored 'channel' systems should be merged into one, filed as a comment on item-11e84d01.
questions: Which are the three separate systems and their separate stores?
What is the content of item-11e84d01 and the tension it described?
What is the scope of the dragnet-development channel?
Is there a plan or decision to actually unify the systems, and who owns it?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a note/comment, and the content matches that purpose: it records an opinion on a prior architectural tension.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to capture agreement that a three-way system split is undesirable and to push toward a single consolidated design.
contribution: It contributes a recorded opinion that unification is desirable and flags a 'channel' naming collision, which a fused system would keep as context for why consolidation is being considered.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim commenting on item-11e84d01, stating that three separate systems that mirror each other's shapes and share the name 'channel' should be reduced to one.
intention_for_embedding: To record agreement with an earlier architectural tension and advocate merging three channel-like systems into a single system.
